one_line: Pure neutral, Inter throughout, sidebar + content + TOC layout. Readable, navigable, no flourish. Feels like Notion, GitBook, Linear docs, Mintlify.

accent.role: Accent IS the text color — inverted button uses bg=#1C1917, color=white. No colored accent.

link.role: Standard link blue. Documentation has decades of convention for links — don't reinvent.

rules: Pure neutral palette. The point is that the CONTENT is colorful (callout blocks, code highlights), not the chrome
Links are standard blue. Docs users have 30 years of muscle memory for this — don't be clever
Callout boxes use pastel backgrounds (info=blue, warn=yellow, danger=red, success=green) but at very low saturation
Buttons are inverted (black on white in light, white on black in dark). No colored CTAs in docs surfaces

mono.family: JetBrains Mono, Berkeley Mono, ui-monospace, monospace
mono.rule: Heavy mono usage — every technical term that could be code IS code-styled

scale.$rule: Bump base back up to 16px — docs are read, not scanned

rules: Inter throughout, no serif. Docs use SANS — readers expect it, serif feels marketing-y in this context
Generous line-height (1.7 prose) — the whole point is reading comfort
Headlines: weight 600, NOT 700+ — restrained authority
Inline code: surface_subtle bg + 1px border, small radius, mono, slightly smaller than body
Code blocks: surface_subtle bg, full padding, syntax highlighting (use Shiki or Prism)
Content max-width: 720-800px (reading-optimized, not full bleed)
Between paragraphs: 16px
Between sections (h2): 48px above, 24px below
Sidebar items: 4-8px gap (dense nav)
Small radii — docs aesthetic is precise, not playful
Code blocks: sm-md (4-6px)
Inline code: sm (4px)
Callout boxes: md (6px)
Almost no shadows. Borders carry depth.
Code blocks: 1px border, no shadow
Modals (rare in docs): 0 0 0 1px border + 0 8px 24px subtle

button_primary.rule: Black-on-white (or white-on-black in dark). Inverted. No accent color.

code_block.rule: Include language label at top-right of block in text_muted. Optional copy button.

callout_info.icon: info icon at top-left in info color

table_of_contents.rule: Right sidebar (200px wide), sticky, hierarchical (h2 indented from h1, h3 indented from h2). Active section highlighted via 2px left border in accent.

sidebar_nav.rule: Left sidebar (260-280px). Sections in weight 600, items in weight 400. Active item: surface_active bg + weight 500.

docs_page.structure: Three columns: left sidebar (260px nav), content (max 720px center), right TOC (200px). Top: searchbar with ⌘K hint, brand mark left.
do: Prev/next page links at end of content

docs_landing.structure: Brand mark + searchbar at top. Grid of category cards (3-up or 4-up). Each card: icon, category name, 1-line description.
do: Show what's new at the top (changelog teaser)

api_reference.structure: Left nav, middle: prose description of endpoint, RIGHT (sticky): code examples in a panel. Two-pane layout for API refs (Stripe-style).
do: Language tabs at top of code panel (curl / JS / Python / Go)

animations.philosophy: The page is for READING. Motion distracts. Use almost none.
anti_patterns: page-load animations on every navigation (reading flow killer)
